metadata.description: azd environment name. Used to scope and tag all resources.
Optional extra tags merged onto every resource.
SSH public key for all Linux VMs. Required.
Pre-shared key for the IPsec tunnels. Required.
CIDR allowed to SSH to the edge VM. Default open; tighten for non-lab use.
BGP ASN for the simulated customer side. Must not equal Azure VWAN ASN (65515).

variables.$fxv#0:
#cloud-config
package_update: true
package_upgrade: false
packages:
  - strongswan
  - frr
  - frr-pythontools
  - iperf3
  - traceroute
  - mtr-tiny
  - tcpdump
  - jq
  - net-tools

write_files:
  - path: /etc/sysctl.d/99-vwan-edge.conf
    content: |
      net.ipv4.ip_forward=1
      net.ipv4.conf.all.rp_filter=2
      net.ipv4.conf.default.rp_filter=2

  - path: /etc/ipsec.conf
    content: |
      config setup
        charondebug="ike 1, knl 1, cfg 0"
        uniqueids=no
      conn %default
        keyexchange=ikev2
        authby=psk
        type=tunnel
        ike=aes256-sha256-modp2048,aes256-sha384-modp2048,aes256-sha256-modp1024,aes256-sha384-modp1024,aes256-sha1-modp1024,aes128-sha256-modp1024,aes128-sha1-modp1024,3des-sha1-modp1024
        esp=aes256-sha256,aes256-sha1,aes128-sha256,aes128-sha1,3des-sha1
        keyingtries=%forever
        auto=start
        closeaction=restart
        dpdaction=restart
        dpddelay=10s
        dpdtimeout=30s
        leftsubnet=0.0.0.0/0
        rightsubnet=0.0.0.0/0
      conn azure-0
        left=%defaultroute
        leftid=__EDGE_PUBLIC_IP__
        right=__AZ_PUB_IP_0__
        rightid=__AZ_PUB_IP_0__
        mark=100
      conn azure-1
        left=%defaultroute
        leftid=__EDGE_PUBLIC_IP__
        right=__AZ_PUB_IP_1__
        rightid=__AZ_PUB_IP_1__
        mark=200

  - path: /etc/ipsec.secrets
    permissions: '0600'
    content: |
      : PSK "__VPN_PSK__"

  - path: /etc/strongswan.d/vti-no-routes.conf
    content: |
      charon {
        # Route-based VTI tunnels: don't let charon install its own catch-all
        # routes in table 220 (they would override the per-VTI routes).
        install_routes = no
      }

  - path: /usr/local/sbin/setup-vti.sh
    permissions: '0755'
    content: |
      #!/bin/bash
      set -euo pipefail
      modprobe ip_vti || true
      EDGE_PRIV_IP=$(ip -4 -o addr show eth0 | awk '{print $4}' | cut -d/ -f1)
      ip link show vti10 >/dev/null 2>&1 || \
        ip link add vti10 type vti local "$EDGE_PRIV_IP" remote __AZ_PUB_IP_0__ key 100
      ip link set vti10 up mtu 1400
      ip -4 addr replace __VTI10_ADDR__/30 dev vti10
      sysctl -w net.ipv4.conf.vti10.disable_policy=1
      sysctl -w net.ipv4.conf.vti10.rp_filter=2
      ip link show vti11 >/dev/null 2>&1 || \
        ip link add vti11 type vti local "$EDGE_PRIV_IP" remote __AZ_PUB_IP_1__ key 200
      ip link set vti11 up mtu 1400
      ip -4 addr replace __VTI11_ADDR__/30 dev vti11
      sysctl -w net.ipv4.conf.vti11.disable_policy=1
      sysctl -w net.ipv4.conf.vti11.rp_filter=2
      ip route replace __AZ_BGP_IP_0__/32 dev vti10
      ip route replace __AZ_BGP_IP_1__/32 dev vti11
      # Static /16 route so FRR originates __CUSTOMER_CIDR__ into BGP (the network
      # statement needs a matching route) and the edge can forward to all customer
      # subnets. No SNAT is needed: the customer test VM shares the edge's subnet, and
      # Azure delivers NVA-forwarded foreign-source packets within the same subnet.
      DEFAULT_GW=$(ip route show default | awk '{print $3; exit}')
      [ -n "$DEFAULT_GW" ] && ip route replace __CUSTOMER_CIDR__ via "$DEFAULT_GW" dev eth0

  - path: /etc/systemd/system/vwan-vti.service
    content: |
      [Unit]
      Description=VWAN VTI interfaces
      After=network-online.target
      Wants=network-online.target
      Before=strongswan-starter.service strongswan.service frr.service
      [Service]
      Type=oneshot
      ExecStart=/usr/local/sbin/setup-vti.sh
      RemainAfterExit=yes
      [Install]
      WantedBy=multi-user.target

  - path: /etc/frr/daemons
    content: |
      bgpd=yes
      ospfd=no
      ospf6d=no
      ripd=no
      ripngd=no
      isisd=no
      pimd=no
      ldpd=no
      nhrpd=no
      eigrpd=no
      babeld=no
      sharpd=no
      pbrd=no
      bfdd=no
      fabricd=no
      vtysh_enable=yes
      zebra_options="  -A 127.0.0.1 -s 90000000"
      bgpd_options="   -A 127.0.0.1"

  - path: /etc/frr/frr.conf
    permissions: '0640'
    owner: frr:frr
    content: |
      frr defaults traditional
      hostname linux-edge
      log syslog informational
      service integrated-vtysh-config
      !
      router bgp __LOCAL_ASN__
       bgp router-id __VTI10_ADDR__
       no bgp ebgp-requires-policy
       neighbor __AZ_BGP_IP_0__ remote-as __AZ_ASN__
       neighbor __AZ_BGP_IP_0__ ebgp-multihop 2
       neighbor __AZ_BGP_IP_0__ update-source vti10
       neighbor __AZ_BGP_IP_0__ timers 10 30
       neighbor __AZ_BGP_IP_1__ remote-as __AZ_ASN__
       neighbor __AZ_BGP_IP_1__ ebgp-multihop 2
       neighbor __AZ_BGP_IP_1__ update-source vti11
       neighbor __AZ_BGP_IP_1__ timers 10 30
       !
       address-family ipv4 unicast
        network __CUSTOMER_CIDR__
        neighbor __AZ_BGP_IP_0__ activate
        neighbor __AZ_BGP_IP_0__ soft-reconfiguration inbound
        neighbor __AZ_BGP_IP_1__ activate
        neighbor __AZ_BGP_IP_1__ soft-reconfiguration inbound
       exit-address-family
      !
      line vty
      !

runcmd:
  - sysctl --system
  - systemctl daemon-reload
  - systemctl enable --now vwan-vti.service
  - systemctl enable strongswan-starter 2>/dev/null || systemctl enable strongswan 2>/dev/null || true
  - ipsec restart || true
  - sleep 5
  - systemctl enable --now frr
  - systemctl restart frr
  - ipsec status > /var/log/ipsec-bootstatus.log 2>&1 || true
  - vtysh -c 'show ip bgp summary' > /var/log/bgp-bootstatus.log 2>&1 || true
variables.$fxv#1:
#cloud-config package_update: true package_upgrade: false packages: - iperf3 - nginx - traceroute - mtr-tiny - curl - jq write_files: - path: /etc/systemd/system/iperf3-server.service content: | [Unit] Description=iperf3 server After=network.target [Service] ExecStart=/usr/bin/iperf3 -s -p 5201 Restart=always [Install] WantedBy=multi-user.target - path: /var/www/html/index.html content: |

__SIDE_NAME__ test VM

If you can read this from the other side, S2S is working.

- path: /usr/local/bin/s2s-validate.sh permissions: '0755' content: | #!/bin/bash LOG=/var/log/s2s-validation.log PEER=__PEER_IP__ LOCAL=__LOCAL_IP__ SIDE=__SIDE_NAME__ { echo "==== $(date -u +%FT%TZ) S2S validation from $SIDE ($LOCAL) -> peer $PEER ====" echo "--- routes to peer ---" ip route get "$PEER" || true echo "--- ping ---" ping -c 4 -W 2 "$PEER" || true echo "--- traceroute ---" traceroute -n -w 2 -m 8 "$PEER" || true echo "--- HTTP ---" curl -sS --max-time 5 "http://$PEER/" || echo "HTTP FAIL" echo echo "--- iperf3 (10s) ---" iperf3 -c "$PEER" -t 10 -p 5201 -J 2>&1 | jq -r '.end.sum_received | "throughput: \(.bits_per_second/1000000) Mbps, retransmits: \(.retransmits // "n/a")"' 2>/dev/null \ || iperf3 -c "$PEER" -t 10 -p 5201 || echo "IPERF FAIL" echo "==== end ====" } >> "$LOG" 2>&1 - path: /etc/systemd/system/s2s-validate.service content: | [Unit] Description=S2S validation After=network-online.target Wants=network-online.target [Service] Type=oneshot ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/s2s-validate.sh - path: /etc/systemd/system/s2s-validate.timer content: | [Unit] Description=Run S2S validation periodically [Timer] OnBootSec=90s OnUnitActiveSec=2min [Install] WantedBy=timers.target runcmd: - systemctl daemon-reload - systemctl enable --now iperf3-server.service - systemctl enable --now nginx - systemctl enable --now s2s-validate.timer
variables.$fxv#2:
#cloud-config package_update: true package_upgrade: false packages: - iperf3 - nginx - traceroute - mtr-tiny - curl - jq write_files: - path: /etc/systemd/system/iperf3-server.service content: | [Unit] Description=iperf3 server After=network.target [Service] ExecStart=/usr/bin/iperf3 -s -p 5201 Restart=always [Install] WantedBy=multi-user.target - path: /var/www/html/index.html content: |

__SIDE_NAME__ test VM

If you can read this from the other side, S2S is working.

- path: /usr/local/bin/s2s-validate.sh permissions: '0755' content: | #!/bin/bash LOG=/var/log/s2s-validation.log PEER=__PEER_IP__ LOCAL=__LOCAL_IP__ SIDE=__SIDE_NAME__ { echo "==== $(date -u +%FT%TZ) S2S validation from $SIDE ($LOCAL) -> peer $PEER ====" echo "--- routes to peer ---" ip route get "$PEER" || true echo "--- ping ---" ping -c 4 -W 2 "$PEER" || true echo "--- traceroute ---" traceroute -n -w 2 -m 8 "$PEER" || true echo "--- HTTP ---" curl -sS --max-time 5 "http://$PEER/" || echo "HTTP FAIL" echo echo "--- iperf3 (10s) ---" iperf3 -c "$PEER" -t 10 -p 5201 -J 2>&1 | jq -r '.end.sum_received | "throughput: \(.bits_per_second/1000000) Mbps, retransmits: \(.retransmits // "n/a")"' 2>/dev/null \ || iperf3 -c "$PEER" -t 10 -p 5201 || echo "IPERF FAIL" echo "==== end ====" } >> "$LOG" 2>&1 - path: /etc/systemd/system/s2s-validate.service content: | [Unit] Description=S2S validation After=network-online.target Wants=network-online.target [Service] Type=oneshot ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/s2s-validate.sh - path: /etc/systemd/system/s2s-validate.timer content: | [Unit] Description=Run S2S validation periodically [Timer] OnBootSec=90s OnUnitActiveSec=2min [Install] WantedBy=timers.target runcmd: - systemctl daemon-reload - systemctl enable --now iperf3-server.service - systemctl enable --now nginx - systemctl enable --now s2s-validate.timer

customData.value: [replace(replace(replace(replace(replace(replace(replace(replace(replace(replace(replace(replace(variables('$fxv#0'), '__EDGE_PUBLIC_IP__', '__EDGE_PUBLIC_IP_PLACEHOLDER__'), '__AZ_PUB_IP_0__', reference(extensionResourceId(format('/subscriptions/{0}/resourceGroups/{1}', subscription().subscriptionId, variables('coreRgName')), 'Microsoft.Resources/deployments', 'vpngw-deploy'), '2025-04-01').outputs.azureTunnelIp0.value), '__AZ_PUB_IP_1__', reference(extensionResourceId(format('/subscriptions/{0}/resourceGroups/{1}', subscription().subscriptionId, variables('coreRgName')), 'Microsoft.Resources/deployments', 'vpngw-deploy'), '2025-04-01').outputs.azureTunnelIp1.value), '__AZ_BGP_IP_0__', reference(extensionResourceId(format('/subscriptions/{0}/resourceGroups/{1}', subscription().subscriptionId, variables('coreRgName')), 'Microsoft.Resources/deployments', 'vpngw-deploy'), '2025-04-01').outputs.azureBgpIp0.value), '__AZ_BGP_IP_1__', reference(extensionResourceId(format('/subscriptions/{0}/resourceGroups/{1}', subscription().subscriptionId, variables('coreRgName')), 'Microsoft.Resources/deployments', 'vpngw-deploy'), '2025-04-01').outputs.azureBgpIp1.value), '__AZ_ASN__', string(reference(extensionResourceId(format('/subscriptions/{0}/resourceGroups/{1}', subscription().subscriptionId, variables('coreRgName')), 'Microsoft.Resources/deployments', 'vpngw-deploy'), '2025-04-01').outputs.azureAsn.value)), '__VTI10_ADDR__', variables('vti10Addr')), '__VTI11_ADDR__', variables('vti11Addr')), '__LOCAL_ASN__', string(parameters('localAsn'))), '__CUSTOMER_CIDR__', variables('custVnetCidr')), '__EDGE_PUBLIC_IP_PLACEHOLDER__', reference(extensionResourceId(format('/subscriptions/{0}/resourceGroups/{1}', subscription().subscriptionId, variables('custRgName')), 'Microsoft.Resources/deployments', 'edge-pip-deploy'), '2025-04-01').outputs.publicIpAddress.value), '__VPN_PSK__', parameters('vpnSharedKey'))]

VALIDATION_COMMAND.value: [format('az vm run-command invoke -g {0} -n {1} --command-id RunShellScript --scripts "tail -n 80 /var/log/s2s-validation.log"', variables('coreRgName'), variables('azTestVmName'))]
